title: fagraea renae
intro: Fagraea renae, also known as the frangipani or strumpet tree, is a tropical flowering plant native to the Pacific islands and Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Fagraea renae lie in its bark, roots, and leaves, which have been used traditionally to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Fagraea renae include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Fagraea renae can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
